decision: Image generation is a formal production capability, not a legacy 404 route. Production access requires a valid Supabase session and writable workspace permission before server or browser provider credentials can be used.

credentialBoundary.rule: Provider credentials must not be read from the Supabase Authorization header.

sourceChanges.summary: Replaced legacy production 404 with assertImageGenerationRouteAccess; production route now verifies session, X-User-Id match, workspaceId, and writable workspace permission before calling the image provider.
Browser provider keys now use X-Nexus-Runtime-Authorization; Supabase session remains in Authorization; workspaceId and userId are forwarded.
Composer image mode passes active workspaceId and userId to the image adapter.
Workflow Runtime Lite image nodes pass workspaceId and userId to the image adapter.
Static auth scan now treats /api/image-gen as a formal protected route and verifies runtime credential separation.
Live auth probe now classifies /api/image-gen as protected spoof-only instead of legacy production 404.
Adds request-scoped Supabase Storage upload/download support for generated images.
Adds authenticated artifact binary download through workspace read permission.
Creates the private nexus-generated-assets bucket and workspace-scoped object policies.

localLiveProbe.result: blockingFindings=0; /api/image-gen classified as protectedRejectsSpoofOnly; protectedRoutes=38; warnings=8 expected local legacy warnings

finalPreviewScreenRun.result: Chrome Computer Use verified editor generation, viewer generated-history hydration, and viewer asset download.

remoteStatus.requiredNextVerification: deploy this source or access latest v22 preview with VERCEL_AUTOMATION_BYPASS_SECRET, then rerun strict live probe.

durableStorage.readPolicy: authenticated workspace members can read generated assets

latestVerification.screenGate: Workflow Pro A/B/C operated from Chrome UI, 30/30 foundation pass
notes: R109 editor screen run produced five new durable generated assets after R108 introduced storage persistence.
The front-end generated counter reached 8 and Supabase artifact/storage counts matched at 6 durable objects.

legacyDebt: Pre-R108 generated records can still reference transient memory-cache asset URLs and may not be downloadable after cache loss.
The R108 route supports new durable metadata and memory fallback, but it cannot reconstruct bytes that were never persisted.
